tasks.text: Fix subsequent-run SPEC verification task appending in `main.py:1568` so it runs independently of video-frame verification and cannot reference `spec_vtasks` before assignment. The current block is nested inside `if frame_descs:` and has three broken modes: (a) `frame_descs` empty with SPEC present — spec verification silently skipped; (b) `frame_descs` non-empty with `vcases` empty — `UnboundLocalError` on `spec_vtasks`; (c) `frame_descs` non-empty, `vcases` non-empty, SPEC absent — `UnboundLocalError` on `spec_vtasks`. Hoist the SPEC verification block out of the video-frame branch and initialize `spec_vtasks = []` unconditionally. Tests must cover all three failure modes above plus the happy path (frame_descs non-empty, vcases non-empty, SPEC present).

Replace the text-pipe behavior of `duplo fix` (without `--investigate`) in `main.py:572-592` with an intelligent diagnosis path. Currently each bug is appended as a single `- [ ] Fix: <oneline> [fix: "<oneline>"]` task with no context-gathering or structured analysis. Route through `investigator.investigate()` (or an equivalent lighter-weight diagnosis helper) and emit one or more structured fix tasks per bug based on the diagnosis. Preserve existing `save_issue(...)` calls. Add tests covering: (a) single bug producing a structured fix task, (b) multiple bugs, (c) investigator failure falling back to a task with the error surfaced, not swallowed.

Update stale reference-path guidance in `saver.py:108-109`. The CLAUDE.md generator currently instructs users not to delete `screenshots/reference/`, but accepted frames are stored in `.duplo/references/` (see `REFERENCES_DIR` at `saver.py:1080-1081` and the copy logic at `saver.py:1166-1211`) and website screenshots live at `screenshots/` root (`main.py:1844-1849`). Rewrite the guidance to match current behavior and add a test asserting the generated CLAUDE.md text references `.duplo/references/` and does not reference `screenshots/reference/`.

Decide and document the `.duplo/references/` vs `screenshots/` fallback in `_compare_with_references()` at `main.py:2015-2025`. The function currently reads `.duplo/references/*.png` first and falls back to `screenshots/*.png` if the former is empty or missing. Either (a) remove the fallback and require `.duplo/references/`, or (b) keep the fallback for backward compatibility with pre-migration projects and document it explicitly in a comment plus CLAUDE.md. Whichever is chosen, add tests for both the primary path and the chosen fallback behavior (or its absence).

Add observable diagnostics for intentionally non-fatal failures. Several silent or near-silent failure sites currently drop data without surfacing it: `screenshotter.py:39-53` (Playwright page failures `except Exception: pass`), `fetcher.py:237-247` and `fetcher.py:417-428` (fetch_site and _download_file swallow exceptions), `investigator.py:126-144` (`ClaudeCliError` collapses to empty diagnosis list; summary is retained but downstream consumers iterating `result.diagnoses` see a clean no-op), `saver.py:354-357, 402-405, 496-499` (dedup fallbacks), `video_extractor.py:261-268` (frame hash failures), and the `main.py` crash-snapshot paths at `29-33, 46-61, 124-149`. Add a single append-only diagnostics channel at `.duplo/errors.jsonl` that every non-fatal failure site writes to, with fields: timestamp, site (module:function), category (fetch/screenshot/llm/hash/io), message, and optional context dict. Update each cited site to write a record before continuing. Print a one-line summary at end of run (`N non-fatal failures logged to .duplo/errors.jsonl`) when the file is non-empty. Add tests per category.

Maintain a persistent `## Bugs` section at the top of PLAN.md so duplo mirrors mcloop's contract. Per mcloop's README ("Self-healing apps" section), `## Bugs` has absolute priority: mcloop's `find_next` returns unchecked items from `## Bugs` before any feature tasks, and mcloop itself inserts runtime-bug fix tasks into this section from `.mcloop/errors.json`. Duplo currently appends all bug-sourced tasks to EOF via `save_plan()`, which means duplo-generated bugs land below completed phase blocks and out of the canonical location. Changes required: (1) On first `save_plan()` call for a project, if no `## Bugs` section exists, create one immediately after the intro prose / architecture note and before the first phase heading. (2) Route all bug-sourced task insertions — `duplo fix` output (both text-pipe and post-upgrade intelligent-diagnosis paths), `duplo investigate` structured fix tasks, and gap-detector bug findings — through a new `append_to_bugs_section()` helper in `saver.py` that inserts inside the existing `## Bugs` block rather than at EOF. (3) Feature-sourced tasks (phase plans, next-phase plans) continue to append at EOF, unchanged; `save_plan()`'s existing non-destructive append logic must detect a top-of-file `## Bugs` section and insert new phase blocks *after* it, not before. (4) Never modify, reorder, or delete existing `## Bugs` entries — only append new unchecked items inside the block. (5) If the user has manually placed `## Bugs` elsewhere in the file, respect its location; only create-at-top when the section is absent. Tests must cover: (a) first-run creation of `## Bugs` at the correct position when a bug is reported, (b) appending to an existing top-of-file `## Bugs` without touching phase blocks, (c) appending to an existing mid-file or bottom-of-file `## Bugs` in the user's chosen location, (d) feature task append from a phase-plan generation landing after `## Bugs` and after the last phase block, (e) idempotency — running duplo twice with the same bug input does not duplicate entries, (f) existing checked items in `## Bugs` are preserved verbatim across runs. Update README.md and CLAUDE.md to document the convention and its alignment with mcloop.
